title: EDR Host Isolation Action Success Rate

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=edr sourcetype=defender:action action=isolate earliest=-90d
| stats count as attempts, count(eval(status="success")) as ok by host
| eval rate=round(100*ok/attempts,1)
| where rate<95
description: Measures isolate-host action success rate as mitigation effectiveness evidence.
value: Measures isolate-host action success rate as mitigation effectiveness evidence.
grandmaExplanation: Measures isolate-host action success rate as mitigation effectiveness evidence — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Retry logic review; (2) offline host handling; (3) map to RS.MI.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Microsoft Defender TA.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `defender:action`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Retry logic review; (2) offline host handling; (3) map to RS.MI.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=edr sourcetype=defender:action action=isolate earliest=-90d
| stats count as attempts, count(eval(status="success")) as ok by host
| eval rate=round(100*ok/attempts,1)
| where rate<95
```

Understanding this SPL

**EDR Host Isolation Action Success Rate (RS.MI-01)** — Measures isolate-host action success rate as mitigation effectiveness evidence.

Documented **Data sources**: `defender:action`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Microsoft Defender TA. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: edr; **sourcetype**: defender:action. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=edr, sourcetype=defender:action, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **rate** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where rate<95` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Single value
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
